summary: Harden Conversation Agent orchestration around canonical runtime-control contracts.
rationale: This stack intentionally crosses the Conversation Agent service boundary, runtime-control persistence, Workbench BFF projection, React transcript rendering, and the registered Conversation Agent prompt. These changes must land together so user text is converted into canonical job/request and requirement revisions, workflow starts cannot bypass approved intents, finalization remains deterministic, and the UI contract stays aligned with the backend schema.
line_count_rationale: Both files were already above the production line-count redline. This PR keeps the product contract in the existing service/store boundary and adds regression coverage instead of introducing a parallel orchestration layer during the release hardening slice.
deletion_targets: legacy direct workflow starts that can replace approved job inputs
canonical final summaries polluted by user display instructions
model-tool terminology for deterministic service actions
risks: Conversation Agent prompt changes must preserve the no-model-tools boundary.
Workflow starts must continue to resolve only from persisted approved intents and outbox records.
Workbench transcript and OpenAPI types must stay aligned with backend message and activity contracts.
